name: convert_with_options
description: Convert HTML or a URL to PDF with full control over the rendering output. This is the same endpoint as convert_html_to_pdf but with advanced options for page layout, custom headers/footers, margins, CSS/JS injection, wait conditions, and more. Use this when you need precise control over the PDF appearance such as adding page numbers in the footer, setting A4 paper size, or waiting for JavaScript to render before conversion.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The HTML content to convert, or a publicly accessible URL.

parameters.name: landscape
parameters.description: Set to true to render the PDF in landscape orientation. Default is false (portrait).

parameters.name: margin
parameters.description: Page margins as an object with 'top', 'right', 'bottom', 'left' keys. Values are CSS strings like '20px' or '1in'.

parameters.name: header
parameters.description: Header configuration with 'source' (HTML string for the header content) and optionally 'spacing' (distance between header and content in pixels).

parameters.name: footer
parameters.description: Footer configuration with 'source' (HTML string for the footer content). Use {{page}} and {{total}} placeholders for page numbers.

parameters.name: css
parameters.description: Custom CSS to inject into the page before rendering. Useful for overriding styles or hiding elements.

parameters.name: javascript
parameters.description: Custom JavaScript to execute on the page before rendering to PDF.

parameters.name: delay
parameters.description: Time to wait in milliseconds after the page has loaded before generating the PDF. Useful for pages with JavaScript-rendered content.

parameters.name: wait_for
parameters.description: A CSS selector to wait for before generating the PDF. The conversion will wait until this element appears in the DOM.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: If provided, the PDF is stored on PDFShift servers and a JSON response with the download URL is returned.

parameters.name: webhook
parameters.description: A URL to send a POST request to when the conversion is complete. Makes the conversion asynchronous.
